title: gaura villosa subsp. parksii
intro: Gaura villosa subsp. parksii, also known as the hairy gaura, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the evening primrose family that is native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The hairy gaura is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, and it has been used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to digestive problems. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
